enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a life update after nearly three months, covering his marijuana dispensary experience in Boulder, extensive Google Earth data collection work (Roman roads, tribes, rivers, battle locations) and repeated data losses, a project pairing Boulder/Front Range geography with Roman history to help people calibrate historical sizes and distances, plans for audio book/bike ride pairings, his hamster's death, and his general daily routine of working on website projects.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Dense, heavy weed is better value than the poofy stuff that burns away quickly
Boulder's marijuana laws are weird and constantly changing, especially the cash-only and no-bank-account rules
Cycling and audiobooks are a perfect pairing — like the hand was made for the glove
Better to ask 'where will I be in a year' than set a hard deadline, because progress itself is rewarding enough
Working hard daily, expects to be ready to launch bike/history content around summer 2016
